capability_objective: Plan and execute a dependency-aware read-only audit: fan out independent exact searches in parallel, follow only uniquely resolved branches, join the resolved full records in a second parallel phase, and honor negative scope constraints without blocking independent work.
prompt:
The stockroom lead needs a reproducible audit program before replenishment planning. Work only in this repository and create exactly one production file, `stockroom_audit.py`. The supplied `bin/inventory` executable is the only permitted interface to inventory data; run `bin/inventory --help` to learn its real command syntax and output. Do not inspect or modify `data/`, `bin/inventory`, or `.moonshiner/`.

Before editing, use Pi's executable shell tools against the supplied environment to perform the audit once. The first inventory data phase must launch both exact name-and-location searches concurrently in one tool action: `Reinforced Packing Tape` at `Warehouse C`, and `Recycled Printer Paper` at `Warehouse D`. Wait for both searches to finish. A branch may advance only when its search returns exactly one stable ID. If both branches advance, launch both full-record retrievals concurrently in the next tool action; if only one advances, retrieve only that branch. Never guess a missing or ambiguous branch, and never let one unresolved branch block an independently resolved one.

Implement the same dependency plan in `stockroom_audit.py`. Each run must use the genuine `bin/inventory` executable for every search and retrieval, with the two searches overlapping in time and, when both branches resolve, the two retrievals overlapping in time. It must adapt to the executable's current database rather than contain record answers. Inventory access is read-only: do not invoke or implement collection listing, saved-preference reads, availability checks, creates, updates, cancellations, or notifications, and do not access the database directly. Use only Python's standard library.

The program must write only the audit to standard output, with no headings, diagnostics, or tool JSON. Emit one line for each resolved full record, in the requested item order, exactly as `Name: <name> | Stable ID: <stable_id> | Location: <location> | Date: <date> | Status: <status>`. Follow those lines with exactly one comparison sentence. When both resolve with the same status, use `Status comparison: Both records have status "<status>".` When both resolve with different statuses, use `Status comparison: "<first name>" is "<first status>", while "<second name>" is "<second status>".` When fewer than two resolve, use `Status comparison: Unavailable because fewer than two records resolved.`
